sampling_notes.method: stride-100 unbiased sample (every 100th row); full customer table loaded
sampling_notes.note_guide_correction: Guide estimate of 900K qualifying orders and 30% date selectivity are both low; empirical data shows ~1.47M qualifying orders and ~49% date selectivity

join_order.rationale: Build bitset on tiny 300K customer set; probe 7.3M qualifying orders rows. Bitset indexed by custkey (1..1500000) = 187KB — fits entirely in L2 cache, O(1) probe with zero hash overhead.
Build compact hash map on 1.47M qualifying orders; probe 30M lineitem rows passing shipdate zone map. Hash map built sequentially in single pass; lineitem probe is parallelized.

logical_plan.aggregation_key: l_orderkey (1:1 with o_orderdate and o_shippriority via orders hash map lookup — no compound key needed)

filter_pushdown.applied_at: zone map block skip before scan + row-level filter during scan
during orders scan, after date filter (bitset probe)
zone map block skip before scan + row-level filter during scan
during lineitem scan, after shipdate filter (hash map probe)

data_structures.customer_filter: bitset(1500001 bits = 187KB) indexed by c_custkey value directly — zero hash cost, single bit-test per probe, entire structure fits in L2 cache
data_structures.orders_join_build: compact_hash_map(pre_sized, capacity=next_pow2(1472400/0.75)=2097152) mapping int32 orderkey → struct{int32 orderdate, int32 shippriority}; open addressing with linear probing; built sequentially before parallel lineitem scan
data_structures.aggregation: compact_hash_map(pre_sized, capacity=next_pow2(120000/0.75)=262144) per thread-local map, key=int32 l_orderkey, value=struct{double revenue_sum, int32 orderdate, int32 shippriority}; thread-local maps merged after parallel phase; keying by l_orderkey only (orderdate and shippriority follow 1:1 from orders hash map lookup)
data_structures.topk: std::priority_queue max-heap of size 10, comparator: primary=revenue DESC, secondary=orderdate ASC; O(n log 10) = O(n) final pass over ~120K aggregated groups

parallelism.phase_1_customer_scan: single_threaded — 9MB total data (mktsegment 3MB + custkey 6MB), sequential single-pass, bitset build, ~10ms
parallelism.phase_2_orders_scan: single_threaded — sequential scan of first ~49% of orders file (zone map skip), reads o_orderdate + o_custkey + o_orderkey + o_shippriority; ~7.3M qualifying rows scanned, builds 1.47M-entry hash map; single-threaded to avoid concurrent hash map overhead for build phase
parallelism.phase_3_lineitem_scan: parallel morsel-driven — 64 threads, each thread processes morsels of 100K rows from the qualifying lineitem block range (zone map reveals first ~50% of 600 blocks are skippable); each thread maintains thread-local aggregation hash map (pre-sized 262144); threads operate independently with no synchronization during scan
parallelism.phase_4_merge: sequential merge of 64 thread-local aggregation maps into single global map; for each local entry, probe global map and accumulate revenue_sum
parallelism.phase_5_topk: single_threaded — iterate global map (~120K entries), maintain size-10 max-heap; O(120K × log 10) ≈ trivial
parallelism.synchronization: thread_local_merge — no locks during hot lineitem scan loop; merge is sequential post-scan

index_usage.purpose: skip_blocks_outside_range — orders is sorted ascending so blocks with block_min >= 9204 (all dates in block ≥ 1995-03-15) are entirely skipped; empirically ~51% of 150 blocks skipped, reducing orders I/O from 240MB to ~118MB per column

skip_blocks_outside_range — lineitem is sorted ascending by l_shipdate; early blocks with block_max <= 9204 (all rows shipped before 1995-03-15) are skipped; ~50% of 600 blocks (300 blocks = 30M rows) skipped, reducing lineitem I/O from 960MB to ~480MB across needed columns

NOT_USED — sequential zone-map-pruned scan outperforms random index lookups on HDD; index would trade ~480MB sequential I/O for random access over 60M × 12-byte slot table (~720MB) + scattered position lookups; net I/O would be higher with worse access patterns on HDD

scan_details.customer: single_pass: read c_mktsegment (dict lookup for BUILDING code) + c_custkey; set bit in bitset for each qualifying custkey; fused filter+project
scan_details.orders: zone_map_then_sequential: load zonemap, identify qualifying block range (first ~49% of file), single sequential pass reading o_orderdate + o_custkey + o_orderkey + o_shippriority; fused predicate (date<9204 AND custkey bitset probe) + hash map insert for qualifying rows
scan_details.lineitem: zone_map_then_parallel_sequential: load zonemap, identify qualifying block range (last ~50% of file by shipdate), parallel 64-thread morsel scan reading l_shipdate + l_orderkey + l_extendedprice + l_discount; fused predicate (shipdate>9204 AND orderkey hash map probe) + thread-local aggregation update

physical_plan.memory_pattern: streaming_sequential — all scans are forward sequential reads within qualifying block ranges; hash map probes exhibit temporal locality (1.47M entries = ~24MB for orders map, fits in L3 cache of 44MB); thread-local agg maps are 262144 × ~24 bytes = ~6MB each, one per thread but accessed locally

phase_breakdown.orders_zonemap_skip_and_scan: ~30 ms (4 cols × ~118 MB = 472 MB sequential, single-threaded, HDD may be bottleneck; if page-cached ~10ms)
phase_breakdown.lineitem_zonemap_skip_and_parallel_scan: ~60 ms (4 cols × ~240 MB = 960 MB total, parallelized over 64 threads; CPU-bound with hash probes if data is page-cached)

estimated_cost.bottleneck: lineitem scan I/O and hash map probing; if data is warm in OS page cache, CPU is bottleneck (hash probe of 1.47M-entry map × 30M lineitem rows after zone skip)
estimated_cost.target_comparison: Umbra=46ms, DuckDB=78ms; target <120ms with correct zone map usage and parallel lineitem scan
